title: Quiz: Federated Learning Basics
description: Test your understanding of federated learning fundamentals

questions.question: What is the main advantage of federated learning?

options.text: Faster training on a single machine

questions.explanation: Federated learning enables collaborative training across multiple clients while keeping raw data local, preserving privacy.

questions.question: In FedAvg, how are client updates weighted?
questions.explanation: FedAvg weights client contributions proportionally to their dataset sizes, giving more weight to clients with more data.

questions.question: What does 'non-IID data' mean in federated learning?

options.text: Data is not Independent and Identically Distributed across clients
Data is stored in a central server

questions.explanation: Non-IID means client data comes from different distributions, which is a major challenge in federated learning.

questions.question: In federated learning, raw training data is sent to the central server.
questions.explanation: False. Only model updates (parameters or gradients) are sent to the server, not raw data.

questions.question: What is a challenge specific to quantum federated learning?

options.text: Different noise profiles across quantum devices

questions.explanation: Different quantum devices have different noise characteristics, adding heterogeneity beyond data distribution differences.

questions.question: What is gradient sparsification?

options.text: Sending only the largest gradient values

questions.explanation: Gradient sparsification sends only the top-k% largest gradient values to reduce communication.

questions.question: What is FedProx designed to address?

options.text: Non-IID data by keeping local updates close to global

questions.explanation: FedProx adds a proximal term to penalize local models that deviate too far from the global model, helping with non-IID data.

questions.question: Which are benefits of federated learning? (Select all)

options.text: Reduced communication compared to sharing raw data
Eliminating the need for any communication

questions.explanation: FL preserves privacy (no raw data sharing), reduces communication (only model updates), and leverages distributed resources. It still requires some communication.
